docSrc.biblCit: Rabier, Jacques-Rene, and Ronald Inglehart. EURO-BAROMETER 10 -- OCTOBER - NOVEMBER, 1978: NATIONAL PRIORITIES AND THE INSTITUTIONS OF EUROPE [Codebook file]. First ICPSR ed. Ann Arbor, MI: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research [producer and distributor], 1980.

titlStmt.subTitl: NATIONAL PRIORITIES AND THE INSTITUTIONS OF EUROPE

AuthEnty.affiliation: Special adviser to the Commission of the European Communities
Center for Political Studies, the University of Michigan

serStmt.serInfo: These surveys are the products of a unique program of crossnational and crosstemporal social science research. The effort began in early 1970, when the Commission of the European Community sponsored simultaneous surveys of the publics of the European Community. These surveys were designed to measure public awareness of, and attitudes toward, the Common Market and other European Community institutions, in complementary fashion. They also probed the goals given top priority for one's own nation. These concerns have remained a central part of the European Community's research efforts -- which were carried forward in the summer of 1971 with another six-nation survey that gave special attention to agricultural problems. These themes were of central interest again in a survey of the publics of the European Community countries -- then nine in number -- carried out in September 1973. After 1973, the surveys took on a somewhat broader scope in content as well as in geographical coverage, with measures of subjective satisfaction and the perceived quality of life becoming standard features of the European Community public opinion surveys. In 1974, the Commission of the European Community launched the Eurobarometer series, designed to provide a regular monitoring of the social and political attitudes of the publics of the nine member nations: France, Germany, the United Kingdom, Italy, the Netherlands, Ireland, and Luxembourg. These Eurobarometers are carried out in the spring and fall of each year. In addition to obtaining regular readings of support for European integration and the perceived quality of life, each of the Eurobarometers has explored a variety of special topics. Also, attitudes toward the organization and role of the European Parliament have been explored in each Eurobarometer beginning with Barometer 7 in the spring of 1977. The Eurobarometer surveys have included Greece since Autumn 1980 (Number 14), Portugal and Spain since Autumn 1985 (Number 24), the former German Democratic Republic since Autumn 1990 (Number 34), Finland since the spring of 1993 (Number 39), and Sweden and Austria since the fall of 1994 (Number 42). In addition, Norway has usually been included since the fall of 1991 (Number 36). Note that beginning with Eurobarometer 43 and Central and Eastern Eurobarometer 6, the archival survey titles in these ICPSR series no longer contain a hyphen separating ''Euro'' and ''Barometer'', in keeping with current usage. Other archives may follow different naming practices for this survey series.

citation.biblCit: Rabier, Jacques-Rene, and Ronald Inglehart. EURO-BAROMETER 10 -- OCTOBER - NOVEMBER, 1978 NATIONAL PRIORITIES AND THE INSTITUTIONS OF EUROPE [Computer file]. First ICPSR ed. Ann Arbor, MI: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research [producer and distributor], 1980.

subject.topcClas: XIV. Mass Political Behavior and Attitudes
C. Public Opinion on Political Matters

stdyInfo.abstract: EURO-BAROMETER 10 WAS CONDUCTED BY JACQUES-RENE RABIER, SPECIAL ADVISER TO THE COMMISSION OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITIES, AND BY RONALD INGLEHART OF THE UNIVERSITY OF MICHIGAN. THIS STUDY IS PART OF AN ONGOING PROGRAM OF PUBLIC OPINION RESEARCH SPONSORED BY THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY. THE FIELDWORK WAS CARRIED OUT BY A CONSORTIUM OF EUROPEAN POLLING ORGANIZATIONS IN ALL NINE NATIONS OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY. RESPONDENTS FOR EURO-BAROMETER 10 WERE INTERVIEWED IN OCTOBER-NOVEMBER, 1978. THIS STUDY CONTAINS AN EXPANDED VERSION OF THE QUESTIONS ON EUROPEAN INTEGRATION THAT HAVE BEEN ASKED THROUGHOUT THE EURO-BAROMETER SERIES. PERCEPTIONS OF RECENT CHANGES IN THE EXTENT OF INTEGRATION AND UNDERSTANDING AMONG THE COMMON MARKET COUNTRIES ARE EXPLORED, AS ARE ATTITUDES TOWARD THE FORTHCOMING EUROPEAN PARLIAMENTARY ELECTIONS. EURO-BAROMETER 10 ALSO CONTAINS AN EXPANDED SECTION ON THE PROBLEMS WHICH RESPONDENTS FEEL SHOULD BE GIVEN POLITICAL PRIORITY IN THE COMING YEARS. THESE ISSUES ARE PROBED BOTH IN TERMS OF THEIR IMPORTANCE AND IN TERMS OF WHETHER THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENTS OR THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY AS A WHOLE MIGHT BETTER DEAL WITH THEM. THE PERSONAL DATA SECTION OF THE INTERVIEW OBTAINED INFORMATION ABOUT THE EDUCATION, OCCUPATION, MARITAL STATUS, AGE AND SEX OF THE RESPONDENT. THIS SECTION ALSO ASCERTAINED THE OCCUPATION OF THE HEAD OF THE HOUSEHOLD AND COMPOSITION OF THE HOUSEHOLD.

sumDscr.geogCover: nine countries forming the European Community in 1978: France, Germany, Great Britain, Italy, the Netherlands, Belgium, Denmark, Ireland and Luxembourg .
sumDscr.universe: the population, aged fifteen years or older, of nine nations members of the European Community: France, Germany, Great Britain, Italy, the Netherlands, Belgium, Denmark, Ireland and Luxembourg .

dataColl.dataCollector: The fieldwork was carried out by a consortium of European polling organizations in all nine nations of the European Community. Survey organizations: France - Institut Français d'Opinion Publique (IFOP), Paris; Belgium - Dimarso/Inra, Brussels; The Netherlands - Nederlands Instituut voor de Publieke Opinie en het Marktonderzoek B.V. (NIPO), Amsterdam ; Germany - EMNID - Institut für Markt und Meinungsforschung, Bielefeld ; Italy - Istituto per le Ricerche Statistiche e l'Analisi dell'Opinione Pubblica (DOXA), Milan; Luxembourg - Dimarso/Inra, Brussels; Denmark - Gallup Markedsanalyse, Copenhagen; Ireland (and Northern Ireland) - Irish Marketing Surveys, Dublin; Great Britain - The Gallup Poll, London.
dataColl.sampProc: REPRESENTATIVE SAMPLES OF THE TOTAL POPULATION OF AGE FIFTEEN YEARS AND OLDER WERE DRAWN IN EACH COUNTRY. THE SAMPLING DESIGNS WERE EITHER MULTI-STAGE NATIONAL PROBABILITY SAMPLES OR NATIONAL STRATIFIED QUOTA SAMPLES. 1) NATIONAL PROBABILITY SAMPLES WERE DRAWN IN BELGIUM, DENMARK, AND THE NETHERLANDS. THESE SAMPLES RELY ON COMPLETE LISTINGS OF THE NATIONAL POPULATION, FIFTEEN YEARS AND OLDER, FROM WHICH RESPONDENTS ARE RANDOMLY CHOSEN (IN SEVERAL STAGES). 2) STRATIFIED NATIONAL QUOTA SAMPLES WERE DRAWN IN FRANCE, GERMANY, GREAT BRITAIN (INCLUDING NORTHERN IRELAND), ITALY, IRELAND, AND LUXEMBOURG. TAKING INTO ACCOUNT REGION AND THE SIZE OF LOCALATIES, RESPONDENTS ARE CHOSEN WITHIN SELECTED SAMPLING POINTS TO PROVIDE A REGIONALLY REPRESENTATIVE SAMPLE WITH RESPECT TO THE CRITERIA SEX, AGE, AND OCCUPATION. FOR FURTHER INFORMATION REGARDING THE SPECIFIC NATIONAL SAMPLE DESIGNS, THE NATIONAL POLLING AGENCIES SHOULD BE CONTACTED.
dataColl.weight: IN ORDER TO PRODUCE SAMPLES MORE REPRESENTATIVE OF THE FRENCH, DUTCH, BRITISH, AND DANISH POPULATIONS, A NATIONAL WEIGHT VARIABLE (V6) HAS BEEN CONSTRUCTED FOR THOSE NATIONS. THE SAMPLES IN ALL OTHER COUNTRIES ARE UNAFFECTED BY THE APPLICATION OF THE NATIONAL WEIGHT VARIABLE. APART FROM THE NATIONAL WEIGHT VARIABLE (V6) DESCRIBED IN THE SECTION ON SAMPLING INFORMATION, EURO-BAROMETER 10 CONTAINS A WEIGHT VARIABLE (V7) WHICH ADJUSTS THE BRITISH AND NORTHERN IRISH RESPONDENTS TO THEIR RESPECTIVE PROPORTIONS IN THE UNITED KINGDOM. THIS VARIABLE INCLUDES THE BRITISH PORTION OF THE NATIONAL WEIGHT VARIABLE, BUT DOES NOT AFFECT RESPONSES FROM THE OTHER EIGHT EC NATIONS. THIS STUDY ALSO CONTAINS A WEIGHT VARIABLE (V5) WHICH PRODUCES A REPRESENTATIVE SAMPLE OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY AS A WHOLE WHEN USED IN ANALYSIS. THIS WEIGHT VARIABLE ADJUSTS THE SIZE OF EACH NATIONAL SAMPLE, WEIGHTING EACH NATION IN TERMS OF ITS CONTRIBUTION TO THE TOTAL POPULATION OF THE COMMUNITY. THIS WEIGHT VARIABLE IS NOT NECESSARY IF THE ANALYST IS ONLY INTERESTED IN THE INDIVIDUAL NATIONAL SAMPLES, AND NOT IN THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY AS A WHOLE. THE EUROPEAN WEIGHT VARIABLE INCORPORATES THE NATIONAL WEIGHT VARIABLE, SO THAT APPLICATION OF BOTH WEIGHT VARIABLES SIMULTANEOUSLY IS UNNECESSARY. THE UNWEIGHTED N FOR THE COMBINED 9-NATION FILE IS 8677, THE WEIGHTED N OF THIS FILE IS 8711.47. ALL RESPONDENTS WERE GIVEN WEIGHT VALUES TO TWO IMPLIED DECIMAL PLACES. FREQUENCIES FOR EACH NATIONAL SAMPLE HAVE BEEN INCLUDED IN THE BODY OF THE CODEBOOK FOLLOWING THE QUESTION TEXT AND CODES FOR EACH VARIABLE. THESE FREQUENCIES DO NOT USE THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY WEIGHTS, BUT DO USE THE NATIONAL WEIGHT VARIABLE.
dataColl.cleanOps: THE DATA RECEIVED BY THE ARCHIVE WERE FIRST SORTED AND CHECKED FOR MISSING OR DUPLICATE CARDS. DUPLICATE CARDS, IF ANY, WERE VERIFIED AND THE DUPLICATES WERE REMOVED. MISSING CARDS WERE REPLACED BY MISSING DATA VALUES. THE DATA WERE THEN CHECKED FOR ILLEGAL (WILD) CODES AND INCONSISTENCIES WHICH, WHENEVER FOUND, WERE ASSIGNED TO MISSING DATA VALUES. THE CODEBOOK WAS REORDERED TO PRESERVE THE ORDER OF THE BRITISH QUESTIONNAIRE. (MINOR VARIATIONS IN THE QUESTION ORDER DO APPEAR IN OTHER NATIONAL QUESTIONNAIRES.) INDICES WERE INCLUDED AS CREATED BY THE ORIGINAL INVESTIGATORS. THROUGHOUT THE TEXT ADDITIONAL BRACKETED COMMENTS WERE INSERTED BY THE STUDY PROCESSOR IN THE COURSE OF PREPARING THE CODEBOOK IN ORDER TO FACILITATE NON-SEQUENTIAL REFERENCING AND TO PROVIDE FURTHER EXPLANATORY INFORMATION.

method.notes: FREQUENCIES FOR EACH NATIONAL SAMPLE HAVE BEEN INCLUDED IN THE BODY OF THE CODEBOOK FOLLOWING THE QUESTION TEXT AND CODES FOR EACH VARIABLE. THESE FREQUENCIES DO NOT USE THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY WEIGHTS, BUT DO USE THE NATIONAL WEIGHT VARIABLE.

setAvail.accsPlac: Ann Arbor, Mi.: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research
setAvail.collSize: 1 data file + 1 codebook file + Osiris data dictionary + SPSS data definition statements
setAvail.notes: THE STUDY STAFF FOR EURO-BA ROMETER 10 DEVELOPED AN EQUIVALENT FRENCH AND BRITISH QUESTIONNAIRE FOR THIS SURVEY THESE QUESTIONNAIRES WERE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE NATIONAL POLLING INSTITUTES WHO TRANSLATED THE QUESTIONNAIRES INTO THEIR RESPECTIVE LANGUAGES. THE TEXT OF THE BRITISH QUESTIONNAIRE WAS USED IN CONSTRUCTING THIS CODEBOOK AND IS INCLUDED IN AN APPENDIX OF THIS CODEBOOK. THE OTHER NATIONAL QUESTIONNAIRES ARE AVAILABLE FROM THE ICPSR UPON REQUEST.

useStmt.citReq: ALL MANUSCRIPTS USING DATA MADE AVAILABLE THROUGH THE CONSORTIUM SHOULD ACKNOWLEDGE THAT FACT AS WELL AS IDENTIFY THE ORIGINAL COLLECTOR OF THE DATA. THE ICPSR COUNCIL URGES ALL USERS OF ICPSR DATA FACILITIES TO FOLLOW SOME ADAPTATION OF THIS STATEMENT WITH THE PARENTHESES INDICATING ITEMS TO BE FILLED IN APPROPRIATELY OR DELETED BY THE INDIVIDUAL USER. THE DATA (AND TABULATIONS) UTILIZED IN THIS (PUBLICATION) WERE MADE AVAILABLE (IN PART) BY THE INTER-UNIVERSITY CONSORTIUM FOR POLITICAL AND SOCIAL RESEARCH. THE DATA FOR EURO-BAROMETER 10 WERE ORIGINALLY COLLECTED BY JACQUES-RENE RABIER, SPECIAL ADVISER TO THE COMMISSION OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITIES, AND BY RONALD INGLEHART, THE UNIVERSITY OF MICHIGAN. NEITHER THE ORIGINAL COLLECTORS OF THE DATA NOR THE CONSORTIUM BEAR ANY RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE ANALYSES OR INTER- PRETATIONS PRESENTED HERE.
useStmt.deposReq: IN ORDER TO PROVIDE FUNDING AGENCIES WITH ESSENTIAL INFORMATION ABOUT THE USE OF ARCHIVAL RESOURCES, AND TO FACILITATE THE EXCHANGE OF INFORMATION ABOUT ICPSR PAR- TICIPANTS' RESEARCH ACTIVITIES, EACH USER OF THE ICPSR DATA FACILITIES IS EXPECTED TO SEND TWO COPIES OF EACH COMPLETED MANUSCRIPT OR THESIS ABSTRACT TO THE CONSORTIUM. PLEASE INDICATE IN THE COVER LETTER WHICH DATA WERE USED.

othrStdyMat.relStdy: The Eurobarometer survey series, launched by the Commission of the European Community in 1974 and continuing today.
othrStdyMat.relPubl: Commission of the European Communities. Euro-baromètre 10. Brussels: Commission of the European Communities, January 1979.
Inglehart, Ronald. The silent revolution: changing values and styles among Western publics. Princeton: Princeton University Press, 1977

Inglehart, Ronald and Rabier, Jacques-Rene. Europe elects a parliament: cognitive mobilization, political mobilization and pro-European attitudes as influences on voter turnout, in Government and opposition, vol. 14, no. 4, autumn 1979, pp. 479-507.

fileTxt.format: THE CARD IMAGE FILE CONTAINS 3 DECKS PER RESPONDENT IN A FORMAT BASED ON 80 COLUMN PUNCHED CARDS. THE DECKS FOR EACH RESPONDENT ARE SORTED IN ASCENDING ORDER.
fileTxt.filePlac: Ann Arbor, Mi.: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research

fileDscr.notes: THE DATA ARE AVAILABLE FROM THE CONSORTIUM IN TWO FORMATS: A CARD IMAGE FILE, OR AN OSIRIS DATASET

fileTxt.format: THERE ARE TWO COMPONENTS TO THE OSIRIS DATASET. THE OSIRIS DICTIONARY GIVES THE FORMAT AND OTHER INFORMATION FOR EACH VARIABLE IN T HE OSIRIS DATA FILE. THE DICTIONARY OR DICTIONARY- CODEBOOK IS USED IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE OSIRIS SOFTWARE PACKAGE. T HE OSIRIS DATA FILE IS CONSTRUCTED WITH A SINGLE LOGICAL RECORD PER RESPONDENT. THE OSIRIS DATA FILE CAN BE ACCESSED DIRECTLY BY SOFTWARE PACKAGES WHICH DO NOT USE THE OSIRIS DICTIONARY BY SPECIFYING THE TAPE LOCATIONS OF THE DESIRED VARIABLES. THESE TAPE LOCATIONS ARE CONTAINED IN THE OSIRIS CODEBOOK.

varGrp.var: V1 V2 V3 V4 V5 V6 V7

varGrp.txt: IMPORTANCE AND PREFERENCE FOR DECISION-MAKING AUTHORITY

var.name: VAR0002
var.notes: THIS VARIABLE IDENTIFIES THE RELEASE VERSION OF THIS DATASET.

var.name: VAR0003
var.notes: THIS VARIABLE IDENTIFIES THE PART NUMBER OF THIS DATASET

var.name: VAR0004
var.notes: THIS NUMBER UNIQUELY IDENTIFIES EACH CASE. THE FIRST TWO DIGITS OF THE IDENTIFICATION NUMBER ARE THE NATION CODES (SEE REF. NO. 8). THE LAST FOUR DIGITS OF THIS VARIABLE REPRESENT A UNIQUE CASE IDENTIFICATION NUMBER WITHIN EACH NATION.

var.name: VAR0005
var.notes: THIS NUMBER CREATES A TOTAL SAMPLE IN WHICH THE NATIONAL SAMPLES ARE PROPORTIONATE TO THEIR SIZE IN THE EUROPEAN POPULATION.

var.name: VAR0006
var.notes: THIS WEIGHT IS NECESSARY TO MAINTAIN THE REPRESENTATIVENESS OF THE FRENCH, DUTCH, BRITISH AND DANISH SAMPLES.

var.name: VAR0007
var.notes: THIS WEIGHT IS NECESSARY TO MAINTAIN THE REPRESENTATIVENESS OF THE UNITED KINGDOM SAMPLE.

qstn.qstnLit: ON THE WHOLE, ARE YOU VERY SATISFIED, FAIRLY SATISFIED, NOT VERY SATISFIED, OR NOT AT ALL SATISFIED WITH THE LIFE YOU LEAD?

WHEN YOU, YOURSELF, HOLD A STRONG OPINION, DO YOU EVER FIND YOURSELF PERSUADING YOUR FRIENDS, RELATIVES OR FELLOW WORKERS (DENMARK: FRIENDS OR ACQUAINTANCES) TO SHARE YOUR VIEWS? IF SO, DOES THIS HAPPEN OFTEN, FROM TIME TO TIME, OR RARELY?("RELATIVES" NOT MENTIONED IN GERMANY OR ITALY)
WHEN YOU GET TOGETHER WITH YOUR FRIENDS, WOULD YOU SAY YOU DISCUSS POLITICAL MATTERS FREQUENTLY, OCCASIONALLY OR NEVER?
ON THE WHOLE, ARE YOU VERY SATISFIED, FAIRLY SATISFIED, NOT VERY SATISFIED OR NOT AT ALL SATISFIEDWITH THE WAY DEMOCRACY WORKS IN (COUNTRY)
ON THIS CARD ARE THREE BASIC KINDS OF ATTITUDES VIS-A-VIS THE SOCIETY WE LIVE IN. PLEASE CHOOSE THE ONE WHICH BEST DESCRIBES YOUR OWN OPINION

catgry.labl: THE ENTIRE WAY OUR SOCIETY IS ORGANIZED MUST BE RADICALLY CHANGED BY REVOLUTIONARY ACTION
OUR SOCIETY MUST BE GRADUALLY IMPROVED BY REFORMS
OUR PRESENT SOCIETY MUST BE VALIANTLY DEFENDED AGAINST ALL SUBVERSIVE FORCES

qstn.preQTxt: THERE IS A LOT OF TALK THESE DAYS ABOUT WHAT THE AIMS OF THIS COUNTRY SHOULD BE FOR THE NEXT 10 YEARS
qstn.qstnLit: ON THIS CARD ARE LISTED SOME OF THE GOALS WHICH DIFFERENT PEOPLE WOULD GIVE TOP PRIORITY. WOULD YOU PLEASE SAY WHICH OF THESE YOU, YOURSELF, CONSIDER THE MOST IMPORTANT?

catgry.labl: MAINTAINING A HIGH LEVEL OF ECONOMIC GROWTH
MAKING SURE THAT THIS COUNTRY HAS STRONG DEFENSE FORCES
SEEING THAT PEOPLE HAVE MORE SAY ABOUT HOW THINGS ARE DONE AT THEIR JOBS AND IN THEIR COMMUNITIES
TRYING TO MAKE OUR CITIES AND COUNTRYSIDE MORE BEAUTIFUL

qstn.qstnLit: AND WHICH WOULD BE THE NEXT MOST IMPORTANT?

catgry.labl: MAINTAINING A HIGH LEVEL OF ECONOMIC GROWTH
MAKING SURE THAT THIS COUNTRY HAS STRONG DEFENSE FORCES
SEEING THAT PEOPLE HAVE MORE SAY ABOUT HOW THINGS ARE DONE AT THEIR JOBS AND IN THEIR COMMUNITIES
TRYING TO MAKE OUR CITIES AND COUNTRYSIDE MORE BEAUTIFUL
DK; NA; INAP., NO FURTHER MENTION

qstn.qstnLit: IF YOU HAD TO CHOOSE, WHICH ONE OF THE THINGS ON THIS CARD WOULD YOU SAY IS MOST DESIRABLE?

catgry.labl: GIVING THE PEOPLE MORE SAY IN IMPORTANT GOVERNMENT DECISIONS
PROTECTING FREEDOM OF SPEECH (BELGIUM AND LUXEMBOURG: ...SO THAT EVERYONE CAN SAY WHAT THEY THINK)

qstn.qstnLit: AND WHAT WOULD BE YOUR SECOND CHOICE

catgry.labl: GIVING THE PEOPLE MORE SAY IN IMPORTANT GOVERNMENT DECISIONS
PROTECTING FREEDOM OF SPEECH (BELGIUM AND LUXEMBOURG: ...SO THAT EVERYONE CAN SAY WHAT THEY THINK)
DK; NA; INAP., NO FURTHER MENTION

qstn.qstnLit: IN YOUR OPINION, WHICH ONE OF THESE IS MOST IMPORTANT?

catgry.labl: PROGRESS TOWARD A LESS IMPERSONAL AND MORE HUMANE SOCIETY
PROGRESS TOWARD A SOCIETY IN WHICH IDEAS COUNT MORE THAN MONEY

qstn.qstnLit: AND WHAT WOULD BE YOUR SECOND CHOICE?

catgry.labl: PROGRESS TOWARD A LESS IMPERSONAL AND MORE HUMANE SOCIETY
PROGRESS TOWARD A SOCIETY IN WHICH IDEAS COUNT MORE THAN MONEY
DK; NA; INAP., NO FURTHER MENTION

qstn.qstnLit: NOW WOULD YOU LOOK AGAIN AT ALL OF THE GOALS LISTED ON THESE THREE CARDS TOGETHER
qstn.ivuInstr: (INTERVIEWER: SHOW RESPONDENT CARDS B, C, AND D.)
qstn.qstnLit: AND TELL ME WHICH ONE YOU THINK IS THE MOST DESIRABLE OF ALL? JUST READ OFF THE ONE YOU CHOOSE.

catgry.labl: MORE SAY IN HOW THINGS ARE DONE

qstn.qstnLit: AND WHICH IS THE NEXT MOST DESIRABLE (GOAL)?

catgry.labl: MORE SAY IN HOW THINGS ARE DONE
DK; NA; INAP., NO FURTHER MENTION

qstn.qstnLit: AND WHICH ONE OF ALL THE AIMS ON THESE CARDS
qstn.ivuInstr: (INTERVIEWER: SHOW RESPONDENTS CARDS B, C, AND D.)
qstn.qstnLit: IS LEAST IMPORTANT FROM YOUR POINT OF VIEW? JUST READ OFF THE LETTER OF THE ONE YOU CHOOSE.

catgry.labl: MORE SAY IN HOW THINGS ARE DONE

qstn.qstnLit: IN POLITICAL MATTERS PEOPLE TALK OF "THE LEFT" AND "THE RIGHT." HOW WOULD YOU PLACE YOUR VIEWS ON THIS SCALE?
qstn.ivuInstr: (INTERVIEWER: SHOW RESPONDENT CARD E. DO NOT PROMPT.)

qstn.qstnLit: ARE YOU PERSONALLY VERY INTERESTED, A LITTLE INTERESTED, OR NOT AT ALL INTERESTED IN THE PROBLEMS OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY (THE COMMON MARKET)?

HAVE YOU RECENTLY SEEN OR HEARD IN THE PAPERS, OR ON THE RADIO OR TV, ANYTHING ABOUT THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT? THIS IS THE PARLIAMENTARY ASSEMBLY OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY OR COMMON MARKET. (LAST SENTENCE EXPLAINING THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT NOT INCLUDED IN GERMANY)
IF "YES:" CAN YOU REMEMBER WHAT IT WAS THAT YOU HEARD THEN?
qstn.ivuInstr: (INTERVIEWER: DO NOT PRESS OR SUGGEST OR PROMPT)

catgry.labl: ELECTION OF MEMBERS OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT

qstn.qstnLit: (NOT ASKED IN BELGIUM, GERMANY OR LUXEMBOURG) HAVE YOU REACHED VOTING AGE (18 YEARS OR MORE)? (FRANCE, IRELAND, BRITAIN, AND NORTHERN IRELAND: IS YOUR NAME ON THE ELECTORAL LISTS? )

catgry.labl: VOTING AGE; DON'T KNOW IF ON LIST OR NOT
INAP., BELGIUM, GERMANY OR LUXEMBOURG RESPONDENT

qstn.qstnLit: IN JUNE OF NEXT YEAR THE CITIZENS OF COUNTRIES BELONGING TO THE EUROPEAN ECONOMIC COMMUNITY, INCLUDING (COUNTRY), WILL BE ASKED TO VOTE TO ELECT MEMBERS OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT. EVERYBODY WILL BE ENTITLED TO VOTE. ARE YOU, YOURSELF, FOR OR AGAINST THIS PARTICULAR ELECTION? (SENTENCE "EVERYBODY WILL BE ENTITLED TO VOTE" NOT INCLUDED IN THE NETHERLANDS OR IN THE FRENCH VERSION OF THE BELGIAN QUESTIONNAIRE)
HOW LIKELY IS IT THAT YOU WILL GO AND VOTE (BELGIUM AND LUXEMBOURG: ... EVEN IF VOTING IS NOT REQUIRED)?
WHEN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENTARY ELECTIONS ARE HELD, WILL YOU BE PERSONALLY INTERESTED TO KNOW THE STRENGTHS OF THE DIFFERENT PARTIES IN THE NEW EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT?

var.name: VAR0031
var.labl: IS R FOR UNIF OF EUR

qstn.qstnLit: IN GENERAL, ARE YOU FOR OR AGAINST EFFORTS BEING MADE TO UNIFY WESTERN EUROPE? (IF FOR:) ARE YOU VERY MUCH FOR THIS, OR ONLY TO SOME EXTENT? (IF AGAINST:) ARE YOU ONLY TO SOME EXTENT AGAINST OR VERY MUCH AGAINST?
IN YOUR OPINION, HAS THE UNIFICATION OF EUROPE MADE SOME PROGRESS OR LOST GROUND OVER THE LAST TEN YEARS? (IF PROGRESSED OR LOST GROUND:) TO A CONSIDERABLE EXTENT OR ONLY A LITTLE?
WHICH ONE OF THESE OPINIONS COMES CLOSEST TO YOUR OWN ON THE FUTURE ELECTIONS TO THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT?

catgry.labl: IT IS AN EVENT WITH IMPORTANT CONSEQUENCES WHICH IS CERTAIN TO MAKE EUROPE MORE POLITICALLY UNIFIED
IT IS AN UNIMPORTANT EVENT BECAUSE THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENTS WILL NOT BE BOUND BY THE VOTES IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT

qstn.preQTxt: HERE ARE SOME COMMENTS ABOUT HOLDING THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENTARY ELECTIONS NEXT YEAR. PLEASE SAY, FOR EACH ONE, WHETHER YOU AGREE WITH IT OR NOT, AND HOW STRONGLY YOU FEEL.
qstn.qstnLit: ELECTIONS ARE NECESSARY TO GIVE MORE DEMOCRATIC CONTROL OVER THE COMMUNITY AND THE OFFICIALS IN BRUSSELS. (FRANCE, ITALY: ...OVER THE OFFICIALS OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY IN BRUSSELS) (BELGIUM, THE NETHERLANDS, LUXEMBOURG: ... OVER THE EUROPEAN OFFICIALS IN BRUSSELS)
ELECTIONS ARE NECESSARY TO DECIDE WHAT KIND OF EUROPE WE WANT
qstn.backward: SEE Q137 FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXT

qstn.qstnLit: THEY (ELECTIONS) WOULD LEAD TO TOO MUCH POWER FOR THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT COMPARED WITH OUR OWN PARLIAMENT IN (COUNTRY)
qstn.backward: SEE Q137 FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXT

qstn.qstnLit: DO YOU CONSIDER YOURSELF TO BE CLOSE TO ANY PARTICULAR PARTY? IF SO, DO YOU FEEL YOURSELF TO BE VERY CLOSE TO THIS PARTY, FAIRLY CLOSE OR MERELY A SYMPATHIZER?
AND WOULD YOU SAY THAT THIS POLITICAL PARTY ATTACHES TOO MUCH OR NOT ENOUGH IMPORTANCE TO THE PROBLEMS OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY?

catgry.labl: INAP., CODED O OR 4 IN Q140

qstn.qstnLit: WHEN THE TIME COMES FOR YOU TO CHOOSE YOUR REPRESENTATIVE IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT, WHICH OF THESE WOULD BE THE MORE IMPORTANT IN YOUR CHOICE? THEIR POLITICAL PARTY OR THEIR IDEAS ABOUT EUROPE?

qstn.preQTxt: HERE ARE SOME THINGS THAT ARE SOMETIMES SAID ABOUT THE MEMBERSHIP OF (COUNTRY) IN THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY (THE COMMON MARKET). COULD YOU TELL ME, IN EACH CASE, IF YOU PERSONALLY AGREE COMPLETELY, AGREE TO SOME EXTENT, DISAGREE TO SOME EXTENT, OR DISAGREE COMPLETELY WITH THE STATEMENT?
qstn.qstnLit: THE FACT THAT WE ARE PART OF THE COMMON MARKET IS THE BEST GUARANTEE FOR (COUNTRY) OF POLITICAL AND ECONOMIC STABILITY.
IN THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY (THE COMMON MARKET), A COUNTRY LIKE OURS RUNS A RISK OF LOSING ITS OWN CULTURE AND INDIVIDUALITY.
qstn.backward: SEE Q143 FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXT.

qstn.qstnLit: THE MEMBER COUNTRIES OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY INCLUDING (COUNTRY), SHOULD GO MUCH FURTHER THAN THEY HAVE SO FAR TOWARDS ECONOMIC AND POLITICAL UNION IN EUROPE.
qstn.backward: SEE Q143 FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXT

qstn.qstnLit: WHATEVER AGREEMENTS OR ALLIANCES THAT (COUNTRY) HAS WITH OTHER COUNTRIES, NATIONAL INDEPENDENCE SHOULD BE THE OVERRIDING CONSIDERATION
qstn.backward: SEE Q143 FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXT

qstn.qstnLit: ASSUMING THAT ALL THE POLITICAL PARTIES SHOWN ON THIS CARD WILL PUT FORWARD CANDIDATES FOR THE ELECTIONS FOR THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT, COULD YOU TELL ME PLEASE, USING THE CORRESPONDING LETTERS WE HAVE SHOWN, WHICH PARTY YOU ARE MOST LIKELY TO VOTE FOR? (FRANCE: WOULD YOU PLEASE CHECK THE BOX IN THE FIRST COLUMN CORRESPONDING TO THE PARTY FOR WHICH YOU WOULD MOST LIKELY VOTE?)

catgry.labl: WALLOON PARTY OF REFORMS AND LIBERTY (PRLW)
PARTY OF DEMOCRATIC PROLETARIAN UNITY (PDUP), DP, AD, LC, ETC.

var.name: VAR0045

qstn.qstnLit: SUPPOSING THE PARTY THAT YOU HAVE CHOSEN DID NOT PUT FORWARD A CANDIDATE (OR IF THE CANDIDATE WAS NOT TO YOUR LIKING), ARE THERE ANY OTHER PARTIES FOR WHOM YOU MIGHT CONSIDER VOTING? WHICH ONES ARE THESE? PLEASE INDICATE BY LETTERS. (FRANCE: WOULD YOU PLEASE CHECK THE BOX OR BOXES IN THE SECOND COLUMN CORRESPONDING TO YOUR CHOICE(S), THEN FOLD THE BALLOT AND PLACE IT IN THE BALLOT BOX) (PHRASE "OR IF THE CANDIDATE WAS NOT TO YOUR LIKING" NOT USED IN GERMANY)

catgry.labl: INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
WALLOON PARTY OF REFORMS AND LIBERTY (PRLW)
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FIRST MENTION

var.notes: REF. NOS. 45-48 DENOTE PARTIES MENTIONED BY THE RESPONDENT AS SECOND CHOICES. THE ORDER IN WHICH THESE PARTIES ARE CODED DOES NOT INDICATE ANY ORDERING IN THE RESPONDENTS' PREFERENCES.

var.name: VAR0046
var.qstn: 2ND CHOICE, 2ND MENTIONSEE Q149A FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXTI5, I10

catgry.labl: INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
WALLOON PARTY OF REFORMS AND LIBERTY (PRLW)
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - SECOND MENTION

var.notes: REF. NOS. 45-48 DENOTE PARTIES MENTIONED BY THE RESPONDENT AS SECOND CHOICES. THE ORDER IN WHICH THESE PARTIES ARE CODED DOES NOT INDICATE ANY ORDERING IN THE RESPONDENTS' PREFERENCES.

var.name: VAR0047
var.qstn: 2ND CHOICE, 3RD MENTIONSEE Q149A FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXTI6, I11

catgry.labl: INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
WALLOON PARTY OF REFORMS AND LIBERTY (PRLW)
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
INAP, NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION

var.notes: REF. NOS. 45-48 DENOTE PARTIES MENTIONED BY THE RESPONDENT AS SECOND CHOICES. THE ORDER IN WHICH THESE PARTIES ARE CODED DOES NOT INDICATE ANY ORDERING IN THE RESPONDENTS' PREFERENCES.

var.name: VAR0048
var.qstn: 2ND CHOICE, 4TH MENTIONSEE Q149A FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXTI7, I12

catgry.labl: INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FOURTH MENTION
WALLOON PARTY OF REFORMS AND LIBERTY
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FOURTH MENTION
INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FOURTH MENTION

catgry.country: GER ITA LUX DEN IRE GB NIR
catgry.labl: INAP., NO SECOND CHOICE - FOURTH MENTION

var.notes: REF. NOS. 45-48 DENOTE PARTIES MENTIONED BY THE RESPONDENT AS SECOND CHOICES. THE ORDER IN WHICH THESE PARTIES ARE CODED DOES NOT INDICATE ANY ORDERING IN THE RESPONDENTS' PREFERENCES.

qstn.qstnLit: WHICH OF THE FOLLOWING ATTITUDES WOULD YOU EXPECT A (COUNTRY) MEMBER OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT TO HAVE?

catgry.labl: HE SHOULD SUPPORT THINGS THAT ARE GOOD FOR EUROPE AS A WHOLE, EVEN IF THEY ARE NOT ALWAYS GOOD FOR (COUNTRY) AT THE TIME
HE SHOULD SUPPORT THE INTERESTS OF (COUNTRY) ALL THE TIME WHETHER OR NOT THEY ARE GOOD FOR EUROPE AS A WHOLE

qstn.qstnLit: GENERALLY SPEAKING, DO YOU THINK THAT (COUNTRY'S) MEMBERSHIP OF THE COMMON MARKET IS A GOOD THING, A BAD THING, OR NEITHER GOOD NOR BAD?
AND DO YOU THINK THAT MEMBERSHIP OF THE COMMON MARKET IS A GOOD THING, A BAD THING, OR NEITHER A GOOD NOR BAD THING IN THE LIGHT OF (COUNTRY'S) FUTURE IN THE NEXT 10 OR 15 YEARS?

qstn.preQTxt: HERE IS A LIST OF PROBLEMS THE PEOPLE OF (COUNTRY) ARE MORE OR LESS INTERESTED IN. COULD YOU PLEASE TELL ME, FOR EACH PROBLEM, WHETHER YOU PERSONALLY CONSIDER IT A VERY IMPORTANT PROBLEM, IMPORTANT, OF LITTLE IMPORTANCE OR NOT AT ALL IMPORTANT?
qstn.qstnLit: (HOW IMPORTANT IS) REDUCING THE DIFFERENCES BETWEEN REGIONS OF THE COUNTRY BY HELPING THE LESS DEVELOPED REGIONS?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS) PROTECTING NATURE AND FIGHTING POLLUTION?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS IT TO) TRY AND REDUCE THE NUMBER BOTH OF VERY RICH PEOPLE AND OF VERY POOR PEOPLE?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS) HELPING UNDERDEVELOPED COUNTRIES IN AFRICA, LATIN AMERICA, ASIA, ETC.?
qstn.backward: SEE Q154A FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXT

qstn.qstnLit: (HOW IMPORTANT IS) FIGHTING RISING PRICES?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS) CONTROLLING ACTIVITIES OF THE MULTI-NATIONAL OR INTERNATIONAL FIRMS?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS) MORE SELF-GOVERNMENT FOR THE REGIONS?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS) FIGHTING UNEMPLOYMENT?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS IT TO) DEFEND OUR INTERESTS AGAINST THE SUPER POWERS (UNITED STATES, RUSSIA, ETC.)?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS IT TO) MODERNIZE EDUCATION TO MEET TODAY'S NEEDS?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS IT TO) STRENGTHEN OUR MILITARY DEFENSE AGAINST POSSIBLE ENEMIES?
(HOW IMPORTANT IS) PROTECTION OF THE PUBLIC AGAINST FRAUDULENT SELLING AND MISLEADING ADVERTISING?

qstn.preQTxt: WOULD YOU PREFER THAT DECISIONS ON HOW TO DEAL WITH THIS PROBLEM WERE TAKEN BY THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY AS A WHOLE, OR BY EACH COUNTRY SEPARATELY?
qstn.qstnLit: (WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) REDUCING THE DIFFERENCES BETWEEN REGIONS OF THE COUNTRY BY HELPING THE LESS DEVELOPED REGIONS?

forward.qstn: Q155B Q156B Q157B Q158B Q159B Q160B Q161B Q162B Q163B Q164B Q165B

qstn.qstnLit: (WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) PROTECTING NATURE AND FIGHTING POLLUTION?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS TO) TRY AND REDUCE THE NUMBER BOTH OF VERY RICH PEOPLE AND OF VERY POOR PEOPLE?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISONS FOR) HELPING UNDER DEVELOPED COUNTRIES IN AFRICA, LATIN AMERICA, ASIA, ETC
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) FIGHTING RISING PRICES?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) CONTROLLING ACTIVITIES OF THE MULTI-NATIONAL OR INTERNATIONAL FIRMS?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) MORE SELF-GOVERNMENT FOR THE REGIONS?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) FIGHTING UNEMPLOYMENT?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) DEFEND(ING) OUR INTERESTS AGAINST THE SUPER POWERS (UNITED STATES, RUSSIA, ETC.)?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) MODERNIZE(ING) EDUCATION TO MEET TODAY'S NEEDS?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) STRENGTH(ENING) OUR MILITARY DEFENSE AGAINST POSSIBLE ENEMIES?
(WHO SHOULD MAKE DECISIONS FOR) PROTECT(ION) OF THE PUBLIC AGAINST FRAUDULENT SELLING AND MISLEADING ADVERTISING?

OVER THE LAST 12 MONTHS, WOULD YOU SAY, IN YOUR OPINION, THAT THE UNDERSTANDING BETWEEN THE COUNTRIES OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY (THE COMMON MARKET) HAS, GENERALLY SPEAKING, PROGRESSED, LOST GROUND, OR HAS STAYED THE SAME?

SOME PEOPLE CONSIDER THE COMMON MARKET AS BEING A FIRST STEP TOWARDS A CLOSER UNION BETWEEN THE MEMBER STATES. PERSONALLY, DO YOU YOURSELF THINK THE MOVEMENT TOWARDS THE UNIFICATION OF EUROPE SHOULD BE SPEEDED UP, SLOWED DOWN OR CONTINUED AS IT IS AT PRESENT?

catgry.labl: CONTINUED AS IT IS AT PRESENT

qstn.qstnLit: AND, IN YOUR OPINION, WHAT IS THE PRESENT ATTITUDE OF THE (COUNTRY'S) GOVERNMENT TOWARDS THE UNIFICATION OF EUROPE?

catgry.labl: CONTINUED AS IT IS AT PRESENT

qstn.qstnLit: IF ONE OF THE COUNTRIES OF THE COMMON MARKET (EUROPEAN COMMUNITY) FINDS ITSELF IN MAJOR ECONOMIC DIFFICULTIES, DO YOU FEEL THAT THE OTHER COUNTRIES, INCLUDING (COUNTRY) SHOULD HELP IT OR NOT?

ARE YOU, PERSONALLY, PREPARED OR NOT TO MAKE SOME PERSONAL SACRIFICE, FOR EXAMPLE PAYING A LITTLE MORE TAXES, TO HELP ANOTHER COUNTRY IN THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY EXPERIENCING SOME ECONOMIC DIFFICULTIES?

TAKING EVERYTHING INTO CONSIDERATION, WOULD YOU SAY THAT (COUNTRY) HAS BENEFITTED MORE THAN, OR BENEFITTED LESS THAN, OTHER COUNTRIES OF THE MARKET BY ITS MEMBERSHIP OF THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY SINCE 1958 (BRITAIN, IRELAND, NORTHERN IRELAND, DENMARK: 1973)?

THREE COUNTRIES FROM SOUTHERN EUROPE, ONE OF WHICH IS SPAIN, HAVE RECENTLY ASKED TO JOIN THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY (COMMON MARKET). IN YOUR OPINION, WOULD THE ENTRY OF SPAIN IN THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY BE A GOOD THING, A BAD THING OR NEITHER A GOOD OR A BAD THING FOR (COUNTRY)?
AND FOR SPAIN ITSELF, WOULD IT BE A GOOD THING, A BAD THING, OR NEITHER GOOD NOR BAD (TO ENTER THE EUROPEAN COMMUNITY)?
qstn.backward: SEE Q172 FOR COMPLETE QUESTION TEXT

qstn.qstnLit: TAKING ALL THINGS TOGETHER, HOW WOULD YOU SAY THINGS ARE THESE DAYS - WOULD YOU SAY YOU'RE VERY HAPPY, FAIRLY HAPPY, OR NOT TOO HAPPY THESE DAYS?

WHEN YOU WERE IN YOUR TEENS (BETWEEN 15 AND 20 YEARS OLD - OR IF YOU ARE THAT NOW), WHAT WOULD YOU SAY WERE THE GENERAL POLITICAL VIEWS OF YOUR FATHER? (NETHERLANDS, DENMARK, IRELAND, BRITAIN, NORTHERN IRELAND: I DO NOT WANT TO KNOW WHICH PARTY HE SUPPORTED, BUT JUST WHETHER HE WAS LEFT WING, CENTER OR RIGHT WING IN HIS VIEWS?) COULD YOU SHOW ME, ON THIS SCALE, ABOUT WHERE YOU WOULD PLACE HIS POLITICAL VIEWS?
DO YOU REGARD YOURSELF AS BELONGING TO A RELIGION? IF SO, WHICH OF THEM?
DO YOU GO TO RELIGIOUS SERVICES SEVERAL TIMES A WEEK, ONCE A WEEK, A FEW TIMES IN THE YEAR OR NEVER?
ARE YOU:

catgry.labl: LIVING AS MARRIED (ALTERNATIVE NOT GIVEN IN BELGIUM AND LUXEMBOURG)

qstn.qstnLit: HOW OLD WERE YOU WHEN YOU FINISHED YOUR FULL-TIME EDUCATION?
IF THERE WERE A GENERAL ELECTION TOMORROW
qstn.ivuInstr: (INTERVIEWER: SAY IF CONTACT UNDER 18, AND YOU HAD A VOTE)
qstn.qstnLit: WHICH PARTY WOULD YOU SUPPORT? (FRANCE: PLEASE INDICATE YOUR CHOICE BY CHECKING THE BOX CORRESPONDING TO THE PARTY FOR WHICH YOU WOULD MOST LIKELY VOTE, THEN FOLD THE BALLOT AND PLACE IT IN THE BALLOT BOX.) (ITALY, IRELAND, NORTHERN IRELAND: "GENERALLY SPEAKING, WHICH POLITICAL PARTY DO YOU FEEL CLOSER TO?")

catgry.labl: WALLOON PARTY OF REFORMS AND LIBERTY (PRLW)

qstn.qstnLit: CAN YOU TELL ME YOUR DATE OF BIRTH PLEASE?
qstn.ivuInstr: (INTERVIEWER: WRITE IN DATE OF BIRTH AND AGE)
qstn.postQTxt: ACTUAL AGE OF RESPONDENT WAS CODED

qstn.qstnLit: HOW MANY PERSONS LIVE IN YOUR HOME, INCLUDING YOURSELF, ALL ADULTS AND CHILDREN?

catgry.labl: NINE OR MORE PERSONS IN THE HOUSEHOLD

qstn.qstnLit: HOW MANY CHILDREN LIVING AT HOME (ARE) BETWEEN 8 AND 15 YEARS?
(HOW MANY CHILDREN LIVING AT HOME ARE) UNDER 8 YEARS?

qstn.preQTxt: WE WOULD LIKE TO ANALYZE THE SURVEY RESULTS ACCORDING TO THE INCOME OF PERSONS INTERVIEWED (THIS SENTENCE NOT INCLUDED IN GERMANY, IRELAND, OR NORTHERN IRELAND)
qstn.qstnLit: HERE IS A SCALE OF INCOMES AND WE WOULD LIKE TO KNOW IN WHAT GROUP YOUR FAMILY IS, COUNTING ALL WAGES, SALARIES, PENSION AND ANY OTHER INCOME THAT COMES IN. JUST GIVE ME THE NUMBER OF THE GROUP YOUR HOUSEHOLD FALLS INTO BEFORE TAX AND OTHER DEDUCTIONS. (IN DENMARK, THE QUESTION IS SIMPLY "TOTAL HOUSEHOLD INCOME." IN FRANCE AND ITALY, "INCOME BEFORE TAX AND OTHER DEDUCTIONS" IS NOT ASKED FOR.)

catgry.txt: FOR BELGIUM, ITALY, AND LUXEMBOURG, THIS CATEGORY ALSO INCLUDES DK/NA

qstn.qstnLit: IF SELF-EMPLOYED OR EMPLOYED: HOW MANY PEOPLE ARE WORKING WHERE YOU WORK? (IN R'S ORGANIZATION, COMPANY, SHOP, FACTORY, ETC.)
ARE YOU THE HEAD OF THE HOUSEHOLD?

catgry.txt: FOR BELGIUM, ITALY, AND LUXEMBOURG, THIS CATEGORY ALSO INCLUDES DK/NA

catgry.labl: METROPOLITAN AREA (BRUSSELS, ANTWERP, LIEGE, CHARLEROI, GHENT)

var.name: VAR0102
var.qstn: WOULD YOU SAY YOU LIVE IN A:(THIS QUESTION NOT ASKED IN ITALY, AND THE DATA FOR ITALY IS MERELY A COLLAPSED VERSION OF OBJECTIVE SIZE OF TOWNV101

var.name: VAR0104
var.qstn: PROVINCE OF INTERVIEW (ASKED ONLY IN FRANCE, GERMANY, ITALY, AND IRELAND)

catgry.country: BEL NL LUX DEN GB NIR
catgry.labl: INAP., BELGIAN, DUTCH, LUXEMBOURGER, DANISH, BRITISH OR NORTHERN IRISH RESPONDENT
catgry.txt: CODE FOR ALL COUNTRIES EXCEPT FRANCE, GERMANY, ITALY AND IRELAND.

catgry.country: FRA NL GER ITA DEN GB NIR
catgry.labl: INAP., FRENCH, DUTSCH, GERMAN, ITALIAN, DANISH, BRITISH OR NORTHERN IRISH RESPONDENT
catgry.txt: CODE FOR ALL COUNTRIES EXCEPT BELGIUM, LUXEMBOURG, AND IRELAND.

qstn.qstnLit: DO YOU AGREE OR DISAGREE WITH THE FOLLOWING STATEMENT: IRELAND WOULD NOT REALLY BE IRELAND WITHOUT ITS IRISH SPEAKING PEOPLE (ASKED ONLY IN IRELAND AND NORTHERN IRELAND)

catgry.country: FRA BEL NL GER ITA LUX DEN GB
catgry.labl: INAP., FRENCH, BELGIAN, DUTCH, GERMAN, ITALIAN, LUXEMBOURGER, DANISH OR BRITISH RESPONDENT
catgry.txt: CODE FOR ALL OTHER COUNTRIES EXCEPT IRELAND AND NORTHERN IRELAND

var.name: VAR0107

catgry.txt: CODED 1 OR 3 IN BOTH Q119 AND Q120
CODED 1 OR 3 IN Q119 AND CODED 2 OR 4 IN Q120, OR CODED 2 OR 4 IN Q119 AND CODED 1 OR 3 IN Q120
CODED 2 OR 4 IN BOTH Q119 AND Q120

catgry.labl: NA; INAP., CODED 0 IN Q119 OR 0 IN Q120

derivation.drvdesc: THIS INDEX WAS CONSTRUCTED FROM RESPONSES GIVEN TO Q119 AND Q120

var.notes: FOR A COMPLETE DISCUSSION OF THIS VARIABLE SEE RONALD INGLEHART, THE SILENT REVOLUTION: CHANGING VALUES AND POLITICAL STYLES AMONG WESTERN PUBLICS (PRINCETON: PRINCETON UNIVERSITY PRESS, 1977)

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE RECODES PARTISAN PREFERENCE (Q262) ACCORDING TO WHETHER RESPONDENTS SUPPORT A LEFTIST OR RIGHTIST PARTY. THE IDENTIFICATION OF PARTY "TENDANCE" IS INCORPORATED INTO THE ORIGINAL VARIABLES. PARTIES CODED FROM 4 TO 39 ARE DESIGNATED AS "LEFT"; PARTIES CODED FROM 40 TO 59 ARE CODED "CENTER"; AND PARTIES CODED FROM 60 TO 89 ARE CODED "RIGHT". "OTHER PARTIES," CODED 90, ARE ALSO PUT IN THE CENTER. THE IDENTIFICATION OF LEFT/RIGHT PARTIES HAS BEEN PROPOSED BY THE PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATORS. OTHER ANALYSTS MAY WISH TO ALTER THIS CLASSIFICATION TO CONFORM MORE CLOSELY TO THEIR OWN RESEARCH INTERESTS.

THIS VARIABLE RECODES PARTISAN PREFERENCE (Q147) ACCORDING TO WHETHER RESPONDENTS SUPPORT A LEFTIST OR RIGHTIST PARTY. THE IDENTIFICATION OF PARTY "TENDANCE" IS INCORPORATED INTO THE ORIGINAL VARIABLES. PARTIES CODED FROM 4 TO 39 ARE DESIGNATED AS "LEFT"; PARTIES CODED FROM 40 TO 59 ARE CODED "CENTER"; AND PARTIES CODED FROM 60 TO 89 ARE CODED "RIGHT". "OTHER PARTIES," CODED 90, ARE ALSO PUT IN THE CENTER. THE IDENTIFICATION OF LEFT/RIGHT PARTIES HAS BEEN PROPOSED BY THE PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATORS. OTHER ANALYSTS MAY WISH TO ALTER THIS CLASSIFICATION TO CONFORM MORE CLOSELY TO THEIR OWN RESEARCH INTERESTS.

var.name: VAR0110
var.qstn: LEFT-RIGHT EUROPEAN PARTY SUMMARY - 2ND CHOICE, 1ST MENTION

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE RECODES PARTISAN PREFERENCE (Q149A) ACCORDING TO WHETHER RESPONDENTS SUPPORT A LEFTIST OR RIGHTIST PARTY. THE IDENTIFICATION OF PARTY "TENDANCE" IS INCORPORATED INTO THE ORIGINAL VARIABLES. PARTIES CODED FROM 4 TO 39 ARE DESIGNATED AS "LEFT"; PARTIES CODED FROM 40 TO 59 ARE CODED "CENTER"; AND PARTIES CODED FROM 60 TO 89 ARE CODED "RIGHT". "OTHER PARTIES," CODED 90, ARE ALSO PUT IN THE CENTER. THE IDENTIFICATION OF LEFT/RIGHT PARTIES HAS BEEN PROPOSED BY THE PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATORS. OTHER ANALYSTS MAY WISH TO ALTER THIS CLASSIFICATION TO CONFORM MORE CLOSELY TO THEIR OWN RESEARCH INTERESTS.

var.name: VAR0111
var.qstn: LEFT-RIGHT EUROPEAN PARTY SUMMARY - 2ND CHOICE, 2ND MENTION

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE RECODES PARTISAN PREFERENCE (Q149B) ACCORDING TO WHETHER RESPONDENTS SUPPORT A LEFTIST OR RIGHTIST PARTY. THE IDENTIFICATION OF PARTY "TENDANCE" IS INCORPORATED INTO THE ORIGINAL VARIABLES. PARTIES CODED FROM 4 TO 39 ARE DESIGNATED AS "LEFT"; PARTIES CODED FROM 40 TO 59 ARE CODED "CENTER"; AND PARTIES CODED FROM 60 TO 89 ARE CODED "RIGHT". "OTHER PARTIES," CODED 90, ARE ALSO PUT IN THE CENTER. THE IDENTIFICATION OF LEFT/RIGHT PARTIES HAS BEEN PROPOSED BY THE PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATORS. OTHER ANALYSTS MAY WISH TO ALTER THIS CLASSIFICATION TO CONFORM MORE CLOSELY TO THEIR OWN RESEARCH INTERESTS.

var.name: VAR0112
var.qstn: LEFT-RIGHT EUROPEAN PARTY SUMMARY - 2ND CHOICE, 3RD MENTION

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE RECODES PARTISAN PREFERENCE (Q149C) ACCORDING TO WHETHER RESPONDENTS SUPPORT A LEFTIST OR RIGHTIST PARTY. THE IDENTIFICATION OF PARTY "TENDANCE" IS INCORPORATED INTO THE ORIGINAL VARIABLES. PARTIES CODED FROM 4 TO 39 ARE DESIGNATED AS "LEFT"; PARTIES CODED FROM 40 TO 59 ARE CODED "CENTER"; AND PARTIES CODED FROM 60 TO 89 ARE CODED "RIGHT". "OTHER PARTIES," CODED 90, ARE ALSO PUT IN THE CENTER. THE IDENTIFICATION OF LEFT/RIGHT PARTIES HAS BEEN PROPOSED BY THE PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATORS. OTHER ANALYSTS MAY WISH TO ALTER THIS CLASSIFICATION TO CONFORM MORE CLOSELY TO THEIR OWN RESEARCH INTERESTS.

var.name: VAR0113
var.qstn: LEFT-RIGHT EUROPEAN PARTY SUMMARY - 2ND CHOICE, 4TH MENTION

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE RECODES PARTISAN PREFERENCE (Q149D) ACCORDING TO WHETHER RESPONDENTS SUPPORT A LEFTIST OR RIGHTIST PARTY. THE IDENTIFICATION OF PARTY "TENDANCE" IS INCORPORATED INTO THE ORIGINAL VARIABLES. PARTIES CODED FROM 4 TO 39 ARE DESIGNATED AS "LEFT"; PARTIES CODED FROM 40 TO 59 ARE CODED "CENTER"; AND PARTIES CODED FROM 60 TO 89 ARE CODED "RIGHT". "OTHER PARTIES," CODED 90, ARE ALSO PUT IN THE CENTER. THE IDENTIFICATION OF LEFT/RIGHT PARTIES HAS BEEN PROPOSED BY THE PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATORS. OTHER ANALYSTS MAY WISH TO ALTER THIS CLASSIFICATION TO CONFORM MORE CLOSELY TO THEIR OWN RESEARCH INTERESTS
THIS VARIABLE, BASED ON Q147, COLLAPSES THE RESPONDENT'S PARTY CHOICE INTO THE APPROPRIATE PARLIAMENTARY GROUP IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT.
THIS VARIABLE, BASED ON Q149A, COLLAPSES THE RESPONDENT'S PARTY CHOICE INTO THE APPROPRIATE PARLIAMENTARY GROUP IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT.
THIS VARIABLE, BASED ON Q.149B, COLLAPSES THE RESPONDENT'S PARTY CHOICE INTO THE APPROPRIATE PARLIAMENTARY GROUP IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT

catgry.labl: DK; NA; NO SECOND CHOICE - THIRD MENTION

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE, BASED ON Q149C, COLLAPSES THE RESPONDENT'S PARTY CHOICE INTO THE APPROPRIATE PARLIAMENTARY GROUP IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT.

catgry.labl: DK; NA; NO SECOND CHOICE - FOURTH MENTION

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE, BASED ON Q149D, COLLAPSES THE RESPONDENT'S PARTY CHOICE INTO THE APPROPRIATE PARLIAMENTARY GROUP IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT.

var.name: VAR0119

catgry.txt: CODED 3, 4 OR 0 IN Q113 AND CODED 3 OR 0 IN Q114
CODED 3, 4 OR 0 IN Q113 AND AND CODED 2 IN Q114; OR CODED 1 OR 2 IN Q113 AND CODED 3 OR 0 IN Q114
CODED 3, 4 OR 0 IN Q113 AND CODED 1 IN Q114; OR CODED 1 OR 2 IN Q113 AND CODED 2 IN Q114
CODED 1 OR 2 IN Q113 AND CODED 1 IN Q114

derivation.drvdesc: THIS VARIABLE COMBINES RESPONSES TO Q113 AND Q114 TO FORM AN INDICATOR OF AN INDIVIDUAL'S POTENTIAL TO TAKE AN ACTIVE ROLE IN THE POLITICAL PROCESS.

var.notes: FOR A COMPLETE DISCUSSION OF THIS VARIABLE AND ITS CORRELATES, SEE RONALD INGLEHART, THE SILENT REVOLUTION: CHANGING VALUES AND POLITICAL STYLES AMONG WESTERN PUBLICS, (PRINCETON UNIVERSITY PRESS, 1977), CHAPTER 11. THIS VARIABLE IS LABELLED THE OPINION LEADERSHIP INDEX IN THE CODEBOOKS FOR BAROMETERS 3, 4, 5 AND 6.

otherMat.txt:
THIS NOTE IS USED IN CONJUNCTION WITH REF. NOS. 114-118 [V114-V118] WHICH ARE RECODES OF Q147 AND Q149A-Q149D. REF. NOS. 114-118 COLLAPSE THE RESPONDENT'S PARTY CHOICE INTO THE APPROPRIATE PARLIAMENT GROUP IN THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT. SHOWN BELOW ARE THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AFFILIATIONS OF THE VARIOUS NATIONAL PARTIES.
FRANCE
EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AFFILIATION CODE NATIONAL PARTY CODE [PARTY NAME]
7 5 UNIFIED SOCIALISTS (PSU), EXTREME LEFT
1 10 COMMUNIST PARTY (PCF)
2 20 SOCIALIST PARTY
2 30 LEFT RADICALS (MRG)
4 40 SOCIAL DEMOCRATIC CENTER (CDS), REFORMISTS
4 42 RADICAL (UDF)
7 50 ECOLOGISTS
6 60 RALLY FOR THE REPUBLIC/GAULLIST PARTY (RPR)
4 70 UNION FOR FRENCH DEMOCRACY (UDF)
4 71 FIFTH LIST
7 80 UDIP-FIDES
7 81 EURO-DROITE
7 90 OTHER PARTY
BELGIUM
EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AFFILIATION CODE NATIONAL PARTY CODE [PARTY NAME]
1 10 COMMUNIST PARTY (PCB)
2 20 SOCIALIST PARTY - FRENCH (PSB)
2 21 SOCIALIST PARTY - FLEMISH (BSP)
4 40 LIBERAL PARTY - FRENCH (PLP)
4 41 LIBERAL PARTY - FLEMISH (PVV)
4 42 LIBERALS (PLB)
4 43 LIBERAL DEMOCRATIC PARTY (PLDP)
7 50 PEOPLE'S UNION (VOLKSUNIE)
7 51 WALLOON UNION (RW)
7 52 FRANCOPHONE FRONT (FDF)
7 53 WALLOON PARTY OF REFORMS AND LIBERTY (PRLW)
7 54 FLEMISH PEOPLE'S PARTY (VVP)
7 55 ECOLOGIST
3 60 CHRISTIAN SOCIALISTS -FRENCH (PSC)
3 61 CHRISTIAN SOCIALISTS -FLEMISH (CVP)
7 90 OTHER PARTY
THE NETHERLANDS
EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AFFILIATION CODE NATIONAL PARTY CODE
1 10 COMMUNIST PARTY (CPN)
7 15 PACIFIST SOCIALIST PARTY (PSP)
7 16 RADICALS (PPR)
2 20 LABOR PARTY (PVDA)
7 22 DEMOCRATS '66 (D '66)
4 40 SOCIAL DEMOCRATS '70 (DS '70)
3 63 CHRISTIAN DEMOCRATIC APPEAL (CDA)
4 70 LIBERAL PARTY (VVD)
7 80 FARMER'S PARTY (BP)
7 81 CALVINIST STATE PARTY (SGP)
7 82 CALVINIST POLITICAL ALLIANCE (GPV)
7 83 DUTCH ROMAN CATHOLIC PARTY (RKPN)
7 90 OTHER PARTY
GERMANY
EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AFFILIATION CODE NATIONAL PARTY CODE [PARTY NAME]
1 10 COMMUNIST PARTY (DKP)
1 15 INDEPENDENT WORKERS PARTY (UAP)
2 20 SOCIAL DEMOCRATIC PARTY (SPD)
4 40 FREE DEMOCRATIC PARTY (FDP)
7 50 GREEN LIST
